Patch Set 3:

well, BANSHEE isn't used to claim existance. this is done by LOHA_EXISTS. this is what you should be clearing in osp_destroy() ?

Patch Set 3:

"well, BANSHEE isn't used to claim existance. this is done by LOHA_EXISTS. this is what you should be clearing in osp_destroy() ?"

Hmm, I think we already knew we can not change LOHA_EXISTS easily once it has been set.  Remember the discussion in LU-6427.

Patch Set 3:

if we set BANSHEE here and destroy RPC get stuck for a while, then any thread trying to access this object gets stuck as well. I think OSP can check object status internally and return an error if it doesn't exist - this is what we do in ZFS/ldiskfs OSD already?

Patch Set 3:

"if we set BANSHEE here and destroy RPC get stuck for a while, then any thread trying to access this object gets stuck as well. I think OSP can check object status internally and return an error if it doesn't exist - this is what we do in ZFS/ldiskfs OSD already?"

hmm, there is no OSD API for checking if object exists. see llog_osd_exists(). As for setting BANSHEE for destroy object, I am not sure I get the point. The object needs to be evicted from the memory, besides we already do this for osd object. why should not we set BANSEE for OSP object. 

Hmm, maybe the whole lu_object_find mechanism needs to be smarter here.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

While I agree with patch, it changes llog behavior on client and need some workaround for that. I think we can postulate that llog_destroy() can't be called from client then we have to delete llog_client_destroy() function.

Alternatively we might check lgh_obj == NULL in llog_destroy() and call lop_destroy() just with th == NULL which will be llog_client_destroy() but I don't see why we need to keep llog_destroy() on client and do such hacks.

commit.subject: LU-6846 llog: combine cancel rec and destroy
commit.message:
LU-6846 llog: combine cancel rec and destroy

Merge cancel llog record and llog object destroy in
a single transaction. Otherwise it will cause the
race between cancel record and llog object destroy,
especally when deleting remote record, i.e. if delete
record RPC arrives after destroy, then delete record
will not find the object, cause LBUG.

Signed-off-by: wang di <di.wang@intel.com>
Change-Id: I33773eeb859fffa871ce01689c5bf1b4196a6658
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/15730
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Mike Pershin <mike.pershin@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
